facts.shortSummary: The party crawls through the root tunnel, loses contact with Holda, and emerges into the Feywild at twilight.
facts.longSummary: After a recap of Holda's contract and the door beneath Dandelion House, the party opens in the narrow root tunnel after Holda has closed the way behind them. Ayveen tries to message Holda to reopen the door and gets no response, Kaito and the others debate what they can still trust, and the tunnel changes from wood to coral- or bone-like material. Tarek compares it to a piece he picked up, the group reaches a mirrored chamber and climbs out through a ruined root house, realizing they have crossed into an otherworldly twilight landscape with distant estates and a road ahead.

location.context: root tunnel from the subcellar to the ruined root house on the far side
location.notes: The party moves from the tunnel beneath Dandelion House into the Feywild region later named the Twilight Kingdom.

npcs.name: Lord Holda
npcs.notes: He hired the party, closed the door behind them, and does not respond to Ayveen's message.

npcs.name: Alden
npcs.notes: The recap confirms Kaito previously detected thoughts on Alden but not on Holda.

npcs.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcs.context: master of the manor beyond the door
npcs.notes: Holda described him only as the man or master of the house; his identity is clarified later in the session.

npcs.name: unnamed elven archivist
npcs.notes: The recap repeats that an elf calling himself an archivist gave Holda the deed to Dandelion House.

items.name: ancient Sembaran contract
items.notes: Tarek's earlier concern is restated: the 400-year-old contract was written in contemporary Sembaran.

items.name: glowing gold key
items.notes: Alden used it in the recap to open the lower locked door beneath Dandelion House.

items.name: bone-like root material
items.notes: The tunnel and far chamber become coral- or bone-like, and Tarek compares the material to a loose piece he found.

organizations.name: Drankorian Empire
organizations.notes: Dandelion House is recalled as a late Drankorian architectural work.

facts.shortSummary: The party follows the road toward the lit manor and finds mutilated human bodies along the way.
facts.longSummary: Leaving the ruined root house behind, the party sees a lit celebration at a manor a few miles down the only visible road through a strange countryside of hedgerows, distant lights, and unfamiliar stars. As they debate Holda's honesty and continue toward the manor, Txarro spots a dead human with green-feathered arrows in the back and a head smashed by a maul-like force after death. The group examines tracks, pockets, and spell components, then continues past a burned pyre with human bones and another stripped corpse, recognizing that the road is dangerous but still their clearest path forward.

location.to: road near Lord Endless Ending's manor (Twilight Kingdom)
location.context: travel by road through twilight hills and hedgerows toward the lit manor
location.notes: The party remains on the road that leads from the root-house exit toward the manor.

npcs.name: unnamed dead wizard
npcs.notes: The human-sized corpse carries spell components, suggesting the dead traveler was probably a wizard.

npcs.name: unnamed dead travelers
npcs.context: additional bodies and burned bones along the road
npcs.notes: The party finds a burned body or bodies and another arrow-shot corpse after the first discovery.

npcs.name: Lord Holda
npcs.notes: The party questions whether he deceived them by closing the door after promising to keep watch.

items.name: green-feathered arrows
items.notes: The first corpse was shot from behind with beautifully crafted arrows bearing bright, unfamiliar feather work.

items.name: wizard's spell components
items.notes: The corpse's pockets hold a writing kit, copper wire, a mouse skull, cotton fluff, and other components.

items.name: mouse skull
items.notes: Tarek keeps the mouse skull from the dead wizard's components.

facts.shortSummary: The party steadies itself, asks for omens, and scouts the manor before approaching the gate together.
facts.longSummary: The bodies sour the party's mood, and Kaito turns unusually earnest in asking Edric for Night Queen prayers and the Wanderer's help finding a path out. As they approach the manor along a high hedgerow, Txarro casts Augury on whether to approach, introduce themselves, and ask the Lord about the treaty; the answer is both weal and woe. The group considers sending Tarek under invisibility, but Txarro instead summons an eastern screech owl to scout the grounds, spotting the silver gate, casual guards, guests, musicians, rowboat departures, and a tall masked figure at the house who sees the owl before stepping back inside.

location.context: road and high hedgerow outside Lord Endless Ending's manor
location.notes: The party stops short of the manor gate while scouting the grounds.

npcs.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcs.context: tall masked figure seen through the owl's eyes
npcs.notes: The owl sees him in a doorway; his identity is confirmed later.

npcs.name: road guards
npcs.notes: The owl sees two casual guards at the gate before the party reaches them.

items.name: eastern screech owl familiar
items.notes: Txarro summons the owl to scout the manor grounds from above.

facts.shortSummary: At the gate, doglike road guards question the party and accept dream-stories as the price of entry.
facts.longSummary: The party reaches the silver gate as the doglike road guards notice them and greet them in heavily accented Common as mortals. Through questioning, the guards say they belong to the road rather than the party, that the bodies came from farther up the road, that the Twilight Kingdom has a dozen dozen houses, and that they keep the road safe from travelers carrying a mysterious bad smell. They explain that the Lord told them to let in any mortal who wanted entrance, ask whether the party brought gifts, and settle for stories of dreams from Txarro, Edric, and Kaito before gesturing the party through the gate.

location.context: silver gate to Lord Endless Ending's manor
location.notes: The party is still outside the manor grounds until the guards admit them at the end of the beat.

npcs.name: road guards
npcs.notes: They sniff for the bad smell, answer questions about the road and Twilight Kingdom, and trade entry for dreams.

npcs.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcs.notes: The guards say he instructed them to admit any mortal who wanted entrance.

npcs.name: unnamed mortal guests
npcs.notes: The guards mention halflings, a dwarf, and other mortals who have come and gone.

facts.shortSummary: Inside the grounds, the party experiences the repeating end of the celebration and begins cautious social approaches.
facts.longSummary: After stepping through the gate, the party sees a sloping lawn, open doors, dimming lanterns, empty glasses, rowboat farewells, and musicians caught in the feeling of a party that is ending but never quite over. Edric tests the boundary by walking back out to the road and returning, confirming the guards do not stop people from leaving. The group agrees to keep one another in sight while splitting into small approaches: Txarro heads toward two halflings, while Kaito and Ayveen approach the tired string musicians, who politely refuse Kaito's offer to relieve them because the Lord was clear and they still have one more piece to play.

location.context: grounds, lawns, patios, and gate area during the ending party
location.notes: The scene establishes the party's looping, unresolved ending.

npcs.name: road guards
npcs.notes: They confirm Edric can leave and re-enter freely.

npcs.name: string musicians
npcs.notes: They play beautifully but are exhausted and bound to perform one more piece.

npcs.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcs.notes: The musicians cite his clear instruction as the reason Kaito cannot take over.

items.name: unusual string instruments
items.notes: The trio plays cello-, violin-, and viola-like instruments with more strings than expected.

facts.shortSummary: Txarro, Kaito, and Edric question two drunken halflings who appear to be centuries out of time.
facts.longSummary: Txarro approaches two half-drunk halflings arguing cheerfully on the lawn, and they are amazed to meet a lizardfolk from Taelgar. They say they are from near Chardon, talk as if Chardon is still on the edge of an empire, misunderstand references to Sembara, Tollen, and the Great War, and insist they arrived that morning by giant rabbit and liberated boat. Kaito joins, uses a flask to coax names and more details, and the halflings reveal that the Lord of the Manor gives brain teasers, is serious and sad, respects or fears his neighbors, and should not be hugged; Txarro concludes they may have been at the party for hundreds of years and leads them toward wine while looking for another mortal.

location.context: party lawn and route back toward the bar
location.notes: The conversation starts near the lawn and ends with the halflings being guided toward wine.

npcs.name: Pandel
npcs.notes: His name is uncertainly rendered in the transcript but Pandel is the clearest spoken form.

npcs.name: unnamed halfling companion
npcs.notes: The second name is not reliably established in the transcript.

npcs.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcs.notes: The halflings describe him as serious, sad, fond of brain teasers, and respectful or afraid of his neighbors.

npcs.name: Rakshasa
npcs.notes: The halflings call him a tiger but say he is not actually a tiger.

items.name: Kaito's flask
items.notes: Kaito uses it to offer the halflings a small drink and keep the conversation moving.

items.name: liberated boat
items.notes: The halflings claim they came to the party by a boat they liberated after riding a giant rabbit.

facts.shortSummary: Ayveen, Edric, and Tarek trade keys to a Rakshasa for the story of Lord Revel becoming Lord Endless Ending.
facts.longSummary: Inside the manor, Ayveen and Edric find a sleeping human and then a sober tiger-headed guest on the back porch who invites them to sit and trade stories. The Rakshasa first asks for a month and a day in Ayveen's home, then accepts three of Tarek's museum keys instead. In return, he explains that he came to the Twilight Kingdom looking for lost things and pieced together the party's story: the neighbors wanted Lord Revel to send mortal guests to their house, failed with gifts and stories, and finally had a wizard challenge him to a naming contest, renaming the estate as a party ending and transforming Lord Revel Beginning into Lord Endless Ending.

location.notes: The beat follows Ayveen and Edric from the bar area to the Rakshasa's porch conversation.

npcs.name: Rakshasa
npcs.notes: He trades information for three of Tarek's keys and identifies himself indirectly through later table discussion.

npcs.name: Lord Revel
npcs.context: former name of Lord Endless Ending
npcs.notes: The Rakshasa says Lord Revel Beginning was transformed by the naming contest.

npcs.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcs.context: current host of the ending party
npcs.notes: The Rakshasa's story explains how Revel became Endless Ending.

npcs.name: unnamed wizard neighbor
npcs.notes: The wizard named the estate as a party ending and imposed the bargain.

npcs.name: unnamed sleeping human
npcs.context: mortal guest sleeping behind a couch
npcs.notes: Ayveen and Edric notice this human while looking for wine and other mortals.

items.name: Tarek's museum keys
items.notes: The Rakshasa selects three keys as payment for his story.

items.name: grandfather clock
items.notes: The Rakshasa says the clock chimes from time to time before Lord Endless Ending sends another mortal down the road.

facts.shortSummary: The party connects Holda's mission to the naming bargain before Lord Endless Ending sends another human away and beckons Edric.
facts.longSummary: Ayveen asks the Rakshasa whether he needs to leave before the grandfather clock chimes again, and he says he has time because other mortals remain at the party. After the party explains that Holda sent them, the Rakshasa advises that shouting does not work and hears Ayveen's theory that the wizard's naming created the truce or bargain Holda wants broken. He confirms it is the only bargain he knows of and suggests Lord Endless Ending may be receptive to ending it; Edric reads the Rakshasa as truthful but self-interested rather than friendly. When Edric searches for another human, the clock strikes zero, Lord Endless Ending whispers to a human who hurries out, then locks eyes with Edric and gestures him closer as Txarro sends his owl after the departing human.

location.context: back porch, bar area, and ballroom
location.notes: The beat begins with the Rakshasa conversation and ends across the ballroom as Lord Endless Ending appears.

npcs.name: Rakshasa
npcs.notes: He gives advice, comments on the bargain, and is judged truthful but self-interested.

npcs.name: Lord Endless Ending
npcs.notes: He whispers to a human after the clock strikes zero and then beckons Edric.

npcs.name: Lord Holda
npcs.context: angry man who sent the party
npcs.notes: The Rakshasa saw Holda's failed conversation with the Lord and says bullying a lord in his own manor was unlikely to work.

npcs.name: unnamed human
npcs.context: mortal guest sent out after the clock strikes zero
npcs.notes: Lord Endless Ending whispers to this human, who then hurries out the door.

npcs.name: road guards
npcs.context: keepers of the roads between houses
npcs.notes: The Rakshasa says they guard what travels the roads, not the party guests.

npcs.name: Pandel and companion
npcs.notes: They do not make it past the bar and can be heard clattering among the bottles.

items.name: grandfather clock
items.notes: The clock strikes zero just before Lord Endless Ending sends the human away.

items.name: naming bargain
items.notes: The party treats the wizard's naming contest bargain as the likely treaty or truce Holda wants broken.
